schema.appDescription: Calculate LTCG and STCG tax on equity, mutual funds, property, and debt instruments. Updated for Budget 2024 rates — 12.5% LTCG equity, 20% STCG equity.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the inputs for the Capital Gains Tax Calculator.

faq.q: What are the capital gains tax rates in India 2026?
faq.a: Equity shares/MF (Budget 2024): LTCG (>1 year) at 12.5% above ₹1.25 lakh/year exemption; STCG (<1 year) at 20%. Debt MF/bonds: taxed at income slab rate (no LTCG/STCG distinction from FY 2024). Property: LTCG (>2 years) at 12.5% without indexation or 20% with indexation; STCG at slab rate.

faq.q: What is the LTCG exemption limit for equity in India?
faq.a: Budget 2024 increased LTCG exemption to ₹1.25 lakh/year (from ₹1 lakh). Any equity LTCG above ₹1.25 lakh is taxed at 12.5%. For example, if you have ₹2 lakh LTCG: taxable = ₹75,000, tax = ₹9,375. You can plan redemptions across financial years to stay within the exemption.

faq.q: Can I save LTCG tax on property sale?
faq.a: Yes. LTCG on property sale (held >2 years) can be reinvested: (a) in a new residential property (Section 54) within 2 years/3 years under construction, (b) in NHAI or REC bonds (Section 54EC) within 6 months — maximum ₹50 lakh, (c) Capital Gains Account Scheme while arranging reinvestment.

faq.q: Is the Capital Gains Tax Calculator free?

faq.a: No. Calculations run in your browser.

examples.heading: Equity MF LTCG — ₹4 lakh gain
examples.text: ₹4 lakh LTCG on equity MF held 2 years. Exemption ₹1.25 lakh. Taxable gain: ₹2.75 lakh. LTCG tax at 12.5%: ₹34,375. Net gain after tax: ₹3,65,625. Effective tax rate on total gain: 8.6%.

examples.heading: Property sale LTCG — ₹50 lakh gain
examples.text: Property bought ₹50 lakh (2018), sold ₹1 crore (2026). LTCG = ₹50 lakh. With indexation (CII 2018: 280, 2026 est: 400): indexed cost = 50L × 400/280 = ₹71.4 lakh, gain = ₹28.6 lakh, tax at 20% = ₹5.72 lakh. Without indexation (Budget 2024): ₹50 lakh × 12.5% = ₹6.25 lakh. Choose whichever is lower.
